title: UN Yemen Envoy Grundberg Briefs Security Council After Riyadh Consultations
detail: UN Special Envoy for Yemen Hans Grundberg briefs the Security Council on April 14, 2026, following consultations with Yemen's Presidential Leadership Council and Saudi Arabian officials in Riyadh. Grundberg reports that prisoner exchange negotiations under the 2018 Stockholm Agreement framework continue in Amman but that progress on a broader political settlement remains limited. The Security Council expresses concern that Yemen must not be drawn into the escalating regional conflict following resumed Houthi strikes against Israel from late March 2026. UN figures indicate 22 million Yemenis require humanitarian assistance, with 18+ million facing severe hunger. The Grundberg briefing cycle — one of the most frequent in the Security Council's agenda — highlights Yemen's status as a chronic crisis where ceasefires hold partially but political resolution remains elusive.

sources.name: UN Office of the Special Envoy for Yemen

media.caption: UN Special Envoy Grundberg briefs Security Council on Yemen peace and regional escalation risks
media.source: UN Office of the Special Envoy for Yemen

title: International Voices Intensify Ceasefire Calls as Sudan Approaches Three-Year Anniversary
detail: As Sudan's civil war approaches its third anniversary on April 15, peace advocates, regional analysts, and diplomatic voices intensify calls for an immediate ceasefire. Analysis details the staggering toll: over 150,000 killed, 14 million internally displaced, and IPC famine conditions in Darfur. White House Senior Adviser Massad Boulos, who leads Trump administration engagement on Sudan, has been finalizing a five-pillar peace roadmap including a proposed three-month humanitarian truce. RSF commander Hemeti signals openness to talks in separate Nairobi discussions with UN envoy Pekka Haavisto, while SAF chief al-Burhan rejects any truce preconditions. The fragmented mediation landscape — U.S.-Saudi Jeddah Platform, African Union AUHIP, and IGAD track — continues to prevent unified pressure on both parties.

media.caption: International calls intensify for Sudan ceasefire ahead of war's third anniversary
